According to the [array api](https://data-apis.org/array-api/latest/API_specification/generated/array_api.reshape.html#array_api.reshape) reshape is supposed to have a copy argument.

### Reproduce the code example:

```python
import numpy as np
import array_api_strict as xps

xps.reshape(xps.zeros((1,2,3)), (3,2,1), copy=True)
np.reshape(np.zeros((1,2,3)), (3,2,1), copy=True)  # TypeError: reshape() got an unexpected keyword argument 'copy'
```


### Error message:

_No response_

### Python and NumPy Versions:

2.0.2

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/comments/1213095511","pull_request_review_id":1455383288,"id":1213095511,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOABkWpM5ITl5X","diff_hunk":"@@ -1128,6 +1128,7 @@ end

 last(p::ProductIterator) = Base.map(last, p.iterators)
 intersect(a::ProductIterator, b::ProductIterator) = ProductIterator(intersect.(a.iterators, b.iterators))
+getindex(p::ProductIterator, inds...) = map(getindex, p.iterators, inds)","path":"base/iterators.jl","commit_id":"d84bc95a4e1277ea70c2b6d0a4ed693e87915ed7","original_commit_id":"d84bc95a4e1277ea70c2b6d0a4ed693e87915ed7","user":{"login":"FelixBenning","id":29106652,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI5MTA2NjUy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/29106652?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning","html_url":"https://github.com/FelixBenning","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"So here is an actual problem with this implementation:

```julia
julia> a = Iterators.product([1 2; 3 4], 1:2);

julia> b = collect(a)
2×2×2 Array{Tuple{Int64, Int64}, 3}:
[:, :, 1] =
 (1, 1)  (2, 1)
 (3, 1)  (4, 1)

[:, :, 2] =
 (1, 2)  (2, 2)
 (3, 2)  (4, 2)

julia> b[1,1,1]
(1, 1)

julia> map(getindex, a.iterators, (1,1,1))
ERROR: BoundsError: attempt to access Tuple{} at index [1]
Stacktrace:
 [1] getindex(t::Tuple, i::Int64)
   @ Base ./tuple.jl:29
 [2] map (repeats 3 times)
   @ ./tuple.jl:250 [inlined]
 [3] top-level scope
   @ REPL[24]:1
```

i.e. map assumes every iterator is one dimensional. The following should be a fix I think

```julia
function getindex(prod::ProductIterator, indices...)
    return _prod_getindex(prod.iterators, indices...)
end
_prod_getindex(::Tuple{}) = ()
function _prod_getindex(p_vecs::Tuple, indices...)
    v = first(p_vecs)
    n = ndim(v)
    return (
        v[indices[1:n]...],
        _prod_getindex(Base.tail(p_vecs), indices[n+1:end]...)...
    )
end
```","created_at":"2023-06-01T12:44:22Z","updated_at":"2023-06-01T12:44:22Z","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/49965#discussion_r1213095511","pull_request_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/49965","author_association":"NONE","_links":{"self":{"href":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/comments/1213095511"},"html":{"href":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/49965#discussion_r1213095511"},"pull_request":{"href":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/49965"}},"reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/comments/1213095511/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"start_line":null,"original_start_line":null,"start_side":null,"line":1131,"original_line":1131,"side":"RIGHT","original_position":4,"position":4,"subject_type":"line"},"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/49965","id":1366659486,"node_id":"PR_kwDOABkWpM5RdZGe","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/49965","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/49965.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/49965.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49965","number":49965,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"add method for `getindex(::ProductIterator, inds...)`","user":{"login":"MasonProtter","id":29157027,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI5MTU3MDI3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/29157027?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter","html_url":"https://github.com/MasonProtter","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Currently, `ProductIterator` has a rather artificial limitation of not supporting `getindex`, even though there's nothing about `ProductIterator` itself that should preclude it from supporting this function.

It's true that `ProductIterator` **may** contain iterators that don't support indexing, but if that's the case, then it should be the job of the enclosed iterators to throw an error saying they don't support `getindex`, not `ProductIterator`.

Here's some examples of things this enables:
```julia
julia> let itr = Iterators.product(rand(3), rand(3))
           itr[2, 1]
       end
(0.17086814961424035, 0.8039107344409738)

julia> let itr = Iterators.product(Dict(:a => 1, :b => 2), [1, 2, 3])
           itr[:b, 3]
       end
(2, 3)

julia> let itr = Iterators.product()
           itr[]
       end
()
```
and something that rightfully errors:
```julia
julia> let itr = Iterators.product(rand(3), (x for x in 1:10 if rand(Bool)))
           itr[1, 2]
       end
ERROR: MethodError: no method matching getindex(::Base.Generator{Base.Iterators.Filter{var"#10#11", UnitRange{Int64}}, typeof(identity)}, ::Int64)
```
Here, I've implemented the most straightforward case of indexing hoping to solicit comments on what this functionality should offer. For instance, this does not currently support things like linear indexing:
```julia
julia> Iterators.product([:a, :b, :c], ['i', 'j', 'k'])[2, 3]
(:b, 'k')

julia> Iterators.product([:a, :b, :c], ['i', 'j', 'k'])[2]
ERROR: BoundsError: attempt to access Tuple{} at index [1]
```
which I think is okay because this object isn't an abstract array. Thoughts on this direction?","created_at":"2023-05-26T17:11:41Z","updated_at":"2023-06-01T12:44:23Z","closed_at":null,"merged_at":null,"merge_commit_sha":"2bf638ee1ee4ab8684852081ec3b64a8713baae0","assignee":null,"assignees":[],"requested_reviewers":[],"requested_teams":[],"labels":[],"milestone":null,"draft":false,"commits_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/49965/commits","review_comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/49965/comments","review_comment_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/comments{/number}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49965/comments","statuses_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/statuses/d84bc95a4e1277ea70c2b6d0a4ed693e87915ed7","head":{"label":"MasonProtter:patch-9","ref":"patch-9","sha":"d84bc95a4e1277ea70c2b6d0a4ed693e87915ed7","user":{"login":"MasonProtter","id":29157027,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI5MTU3MDI3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/29157027?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter","html_url":"https://github.com/MasonProtter","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"repo":{"id":257991408,"node_id":"MDEwOlJlcG9zaXRvcnkyNTc5OTE0MDg=","name":"julia","full_name":"MasonProtter/julia","private":false,"owner":{"login":"MasonProtter","id":29157 method for `getindex(::ProductIterator, inds...)`","user":{"login":"MasonProtter","id":29157027,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI5MTU3MDI3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/29157027?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter","html_url":"https://github.com/MasonProtter","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Currently, `ProductIterator` has a rather artificial limitation of not supporting `getindex`, even though there's nothing about `ProductIterator` itself that should preclude it from supporting this function.

It's true that `ProductIterator` **may** contain iterators that don't support indexing, but if that's the case, then it should be the job of the enclosed iterators to throw an error saying they don't support `getindex`, not `ProductIterator`.

Here's some examples of things this enables:
```julia
julia> let itr = Iterators.product(rand(3), rand(3))
           itr[2, 1]
       end
(0.17086814961424035, 0.8039107344409738)

julia> let itr = Iterators.product(Dict(:a => 1, :b => 2), [1, 2, 3])
           itr[:b, 3]
       end
(2, 3)

julia> let itr = Iterators.product()
           itr[]
       end
()
```
and something that rightfully errors:
```julia
julia> let itr = Iterators.product(rand(3), (x for x in 1:10 if rand(Bool)))
           itr[1, 2]
       end
ERROR: MethodError: no method matching getindex(::Base.Generator{Base.Iterators.Filter{var"#10#11", UnitRange{Int64}}, typeof(identity)}, ::Int64)
```
Here, I've implemented the most straightforward case of indexing hoping to solicit comments on what this functionality should offer. For instance, this does not currently support things like linear indexing:
```julia
julia> Iterators.product([:a, :b, :c], ['i', 'j', 'k'])[2, 3]
(:b, 'k')

julia> Iterators.product([:a, :b, :c], ['i', 'j', 'k'])[2]
ERROR: BoundsError: attempt to access Tuple{} at index [1]
```
which I think is okay because this object isn't an abstract array. Thoughts on this inds...)`","user":{"login":"MasonProtter","id":29157027,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI5MTU3MDI3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/29157027?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter","html_url":"https://github.com/MasonProtter","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Currently, `ProductIterator` has a rather artificial limitation of not supporting `getindex`, even though there's nothing about `ProductIterator` itself that should preclude it from supporting this function.

It's true that `ProductIterator` **may** contain iterators that don't support indexing, but if that's the case, then it should be the job of the enclosed iterators to throw an error saying they don't support `getindex`, not `ProductIterator`.

Here's some examples of things this enables:
```julia
julia> let itr = Iterators.product(rand(3), rand(3))
           itr[2, 1]
       end
(0.17086814961424035, 0.8039107344409738)

julia> let itr = Iterators.product(Dict(:a => 1, :b => 2), [1, 2, 3])
           itr[:b, 3]
       end
(2, 3)

julia> let itr = Iterators.product()
           itr[]
       end
()
```
and something that rightfully errors:
```julia
julia> let itr = Iterators.product(rand(3), (x for x in 1:10 if rand(Bool)))
           itr[1, 2]
       end
ERROR: MethodError: no method matching getindex(::Base.Generator{Base.Iterators.Filter{var"#10#11", UnitRange{Int64}}, typeof(identity)}, ::Int64)
```
Here, I've implemented the most straightforward case of indexing hoping to solicit comments on what this functionality should offer. For instance, this does not currently support things like linear indexing:
```julia
julia> Iterators.product([:a, :b, :c], ['i', 'j', 'k'])[2, 3]
(:b, 'k')

julia> Iterators.product([:a, :b, :c], ['i', 'j', 'k'])[2]
ERROR: BoundsError: attempt to access Tuple{} at index [1]
```
which I think is okay because this object isn't an abstract array. Thoughts on this method for `getindex(::ProductIterator, inds...)`","user":{"login":"MasonProtter","id":29157027,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI5MTU3MDI3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/29157027?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter","html_url":"https://github.com/MasonProtter","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Currently, `ProductIterator` has a rather artificial limitation of not supporting `getindex`, even though there's nothing about `ProductIterator` itself that should preclude it from supporting this function.

It's true that `ProductIterator` **may** contain iterators that don't support indexing, but if that's the case, then it should be the job of the enclosed iterators to throw an error saying they don't support `getindex`, not `ProductIterator`.

Here's some examples of things this enables:
```julia
julia> let itr = Iterators.product(rand(3), rand(3))
           itr[2, 1]
       end
(0.17086814961424035, 0.8039107344409738)

julia> let itr = Iterators.product(Dict(:a => 1, :b => 2), [1, 2, 3])
           itr[:b, 3]
       end
(2, 3)

julia> let itr = Iterators.product()
           itr[]
       end
()
```
and something that rightfully errors:
```julia
julia> let itr = Iterators.product(rand(3), (x for x in 1:10 if rand(Bool)))
           itr[1, 2]
       end
ERROR: MethodError: no method matching getindex(::Base.Generator{Base.Iterators.Filter{var"#10#11", UnitRange{Int64}}, typeof(identity)}, ::Int64)
```
Here, I've implemented the most straightforward case of indexing hoping to solicit comments on what this functionality should offer. For instance, this does not currently support things like linear indexing:
```julia
julia> Iterators.product([:a, :b, :c], ['i', 'j', 'k'])[2, 3]
(:b, 'k')

julia> Iterators.product([:a, :b, :c], ['i', 'j', 'k'])[2]
ERROR: BoundsError: attempt to access Tuple{} at index [1]
```
which I think is okay because this object isn't an abstract array. Thoughts on this inds...)`","user":{"login":"MasonProtter","id":29157027,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI5MTU3MDI3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/29157027?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter","html_url":"https://github.com/MasonProtter","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":7,"created_at":"2023-05-26T17:11:41Z","updated_at":"2023-06-02T09:32:18Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/49965","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/49965","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/49965.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/49965.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"Currently, `ProductIterator` has a rather artificial limitation of not supporting `getindex`, even though there's nothing about `ProductIterator` itself that should preclude it from supporting this function.

It's true that `ProductIterator` **may** contain iterators that don't support indexing, but if that's the case, then it should be the job of the enclosed iterators to throw an error saying they don't support `getindex`, not `ProductIterator`.

Here's some examples of things this enables:
```julia
julia> let itr = Iterators.product(rand(3), rand(3))
           itr[2, 1]
       end
(0.17086814961424035, 0.8039107344409738)

julia> let itr = Iterators.product(Dict(:a => 1, :b => 2), [1, 2, 3])
           itr[:b, 3]
       end
(2, 3)

julia> let itr = Iterators.product()
           itr[]
       end
()
```
and something that rightfully errors:
```julia
julia> let itr = Iterators.product(rand(3), (x for x in 1:10 if rand(Bool)))
           itr[1, 2]
       end
ERROR: MethodError: no method matching getindex(::Base.Generator{Base.Iterators.Filter{var"#10#11", UnitRange{Int64}}, typeof(identity)}, ::Int64)
```
Here, I've implemented the most straightforward case of indexing hoping to solicit comments on what this functionality should offer. For instance, this does not currently support things like linear indexing:
```julia
julia> Iterators.product([:a, :b, :c], ['i', 'j', 'k'])[2, 3]
(:b, 'k')

julia> Iterators.product([:a, :b, :c], ['i', 'j', 'k'])[2]
ERROR: BoundsError: attempt to access Tuple{} at index [1]
```
which I think is okay because this object isn't an abstract array. Thoughts on this direction?","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49965/reactions","total_count":2,"+1":2,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49965/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1573438052","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/49965#issuecomment-1573438052","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49965","id":1573438052,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5dyMJk","user":{"login":"FelixBenning","id":29106652,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI5MTA2NjUy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/29106652?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning","html_url":"https://github.com/FelixBenning","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-06-02T09:32:18Z","updated_at":"2023-06-02T09:32:18Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"> "product-as-array" is available in packages, eg see `RectiGrids.jl`.

help?> Base.IteratorSize
  IteratorSize(itertype::Type) -> IteratorSize

  Given the type of an iterator, return one of the following values:

    •  SizeUnknown() if the length (number of elements) cannot be determined in advance.

    •  HasLength() if there is a fixed, finite length.

    •  HasShape{N}() if there is a known length plus a notion of multidimensional shape (as for an array). In this case N should give the number of dimensions, and the axes function is valid for the iterator.

    •  IsInfinite() if the iterator yields values forever.

  The default value (for iterators that do not define this function) is HasLength(). This means that most iterators are assumed to implement length.

  This trait is generally used to select between algorithms that pre-allocate space for their result, and algorithms that resize their result incrementally.

  julia> Base.IteratorSize(1:5)
  Base.HasShape{1}()
  
  julia> Base.IteratorSize((2,3))
  Base.HasLength()
```

"fixed, finite length" does not imply one based. But this is implicitly assumed in Base (e.g. 
```julia
_prod_size1(a, ::HasLength) = (length(a),)
```
https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/master/base/iterators.jl#L1049) which is probably why 
```julia
julia> Base.IteratorSize(OffsetArray(1:3, -1:1))
Base.HasShape{1}()
```

help?> Base.IteratorSize
  IteratorSize(itertype::Type) -> IteratorSize

  Given the type of an iterator, return one of the following values:

    •  SizeUnknown() if the length (number of elements) cannot be determined in advance.

    •  HasLength() if there is a fixed, finite length.

    •  HasShape{N}() if there is a known length plus a notion of multidimensional shape (as for an array). In this case N should give the number of dimensions, and the axes function is valid for the iterator.

    •  IsInfinite() if the iterator yields values forever.

  The default value (for iterators that do not define this function) is HasLength(). This means that most iterators are assumed to implement length.

  This trait is generally used to select between algorithms that pre-allocate space for their result, and algorithms that resize their result incrementally.

  julia> Base.IteratorSize(1:5)
  Base.HasShape{1}()
  
  julia> Base.IteratorSize((2,3))
  Base.HasLength()
```

"fixed, finite length" does not imply one based. But this is implicitly assumed in Base (e.g. 
```julia
_prod_size1(a, ::HasLength) = (length(a),)
```
https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/master/base/iterators.jl#L1049) which is probably why 
```julia
julia> Base.IteratorSize(OffsetArray(1:3, -1:1))
Base.HasShape{1}()
```

should the documentation be updated? {"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/50051","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/50051/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/50051/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/50051/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/50051","id":1739557047,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM5nr4i3","number":50051,"title":"Does `collect` always return an Array or can it return `AbstractArray`s?","user":{"login":"FelixBenning","id":29106652,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI5MTA2NjUy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/29106652?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning","html_url":"https://github.com/FelixBenning","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2023-06-03T13:46:01Z","updated_at":"2023-06-03T13:46:01Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"The documentation says `Array`
```julia
help?> collect

...
  collect(collection)

  Return an Array of all items in a collection or iterator. For dictionaries, returns Pair{KeyType, ValType}. If the argument is array-like or is an iterator with the HasShape trait, the result will have the same shape and number of dimensions as the argument.
```

 but collect of `Iterators.product` breaks this promise:
```julia
julia> Iterators.product(OffsetArray(rand(3), -1:1), 1:2) |> collect
3×2 OffsetArray(::Matrix{Tuple{Float64, Int64}}, -1:1, 1:2) with eltype Tuple{Float64, Int64} with indices -1:1×1:2:
 (0.892917, 1)  (0.892917, 2)
 (0.398366, 1)  (0.398366, 2)
 (0.550785, 1)  (0.550785, 2)
```

I think returning an AbstractArray is fine if the documentation is updated. Interestingly `collect(collect())` results in a normal Array. So `OffsetArrays` is following the documentation.

method for `getindex(::ProductIterator, inds...)`","user":{"login":"MasonProtter","id":29157027,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI5MTU3MDI3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/29157027?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter","html_url":"https://github.com/MasonProtter","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2023-05-26T17:11:41Z","updated_at":"2023-05-29T09:47:41Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/49965","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/49965","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/49965.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/49965.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"Currently, `ProductIterator` has a rather artificial limitation of not supporting `getindex`, even though there's nothing about `ProductIterator` itself that should preclude it from supporting this function.

It's true that `ProductIterator` **may** contain iterators that don't support indexing, but if that's the case, then it should be the job of the enclosed iterators to throw an error saying they don't support `getindex`, not `ProductIterator`.

Here's some examples of things this enables:
```julia
julia> let itr = Iterators.product(rand(3), rand(3))
           itr[2, 1]
       end
(0.17086814961424035, 0.8039107344409738)

julia> let itr = Iterators.product(Dict(:a => 1, :b => 2), [1, 2, 3])
           itr[:b, 3]
       end
(2, 3)

julia> let itr = Iterators.product()
           itr[]
       end
()
```
and something that rightfully errors:
```julia
julia> let itr = Iterators.product(rand(3), (x for x in 1:10 if rand(Bool)))
           itr[1, 2]
       end
ERROR: MethodError: no method matching getindex(::Base.Generator{Base.Iterators.Filter{var"#10#11", UnitRange{Int64}}, typeof(identity)}, ::Int64)
```
Here, I've implemented the most straightforward case of indexing hoping to solicit comments on what this functionality should offer. For instance, this does not currently support things like linear indexing:
```julia
julia> Iterators.product([:a, :b, :c], ['i', 'j', 'k'])[2, 3]
(:b, 'k')

julia> Iterators.product([:a, :b, :c], ['i', 'j', 'k'])[2]
ERROR: BoundsError: attempt to access Tuple{} at index [1]
```
which I think is okay because this object isn't an abstract array. Thoughts on this inds...)`","user":{"login":"MasonProtter","id":29157027,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI5MTU3MDI3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/29157027?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter","html_url":"https://github.com/MasonProtter","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Currently, `ProductIterator` has a rather artificial limitation of not supporting `getindex`, even though there's nothing about `ProductIterator` itself that should preclude it from supporting this function.

It's true that `ProductIterator` **may** contain iterators that don't support indexing, but if that's the case, then it should be the job of the enclosed iterators to throw an error saying they don't support `getindex`, not `ProductIterator`.

Here's some examples of things this enables:
```julia
julia> let itr = Iterators.product(rand(3), rand(3))
           itr[2, 1]
       end
(0.17086814961424035, 0.8039107344409738)

julia> let itr = Iterators.product(Dict(:a => 1, :b => 2), [1, 2, 3])
           itr[:b, 3]
       end
(2, 3)

julia> let itr = Iterators.product()
           itr[]
       end
()
```
and something that rightfully errors:
```julia
julia> let itr = Iterators.product(rand(3), (x for x in 1:10 if rand(Bool)))
           itr[1, 2]
       end
ERROR: MethodError: no method matching getindex(::Base.Generator{Base.Iterators.Filter{var"#10#11", UnitRange{Int64}}, typeof(identity)}, ::Int64)
```
Here, I've implemented the most straightforward case of indexing hoping to solicit comments on what this functionality should offer. For instance, this does not currently support things like linear indexing:
```julia
julia> Iterators.product([:a, :b, :c], ['i', 'j', 'k'])[2, 3]
(:b, 'k')

julia> Iterators.product([:a, :b, :c], ['i', 'j', 'k'])[2]
ERROR: BoundsError: attempt to access Tuple{} at index [1]
```
which I think is okay because this object isn't an abstract array. Thoughts on this inds...)`","user":{"login":"MasonProtter","id":29157027,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI5MTU3MDI3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/29157027?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter","html_url":"https://github.com/MasonProtter","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Currently, `ProductIterator` has a rather artificial limitation of not supporting `getindex`, even though there's nothing about `ProductIterator` itself that should preclude it from supporting this function.

It's true that `ProductIterator` **may** contain iterators that don't support indexing, but if that's the case, then it should be the job of the enclosed iterators to throw an error saying they don't support `getindex`, not `ProductIterator`.

Here's some examples of things this enables:
```julia
julia> let itr = Iterators.product(rand(3), rand(3))
           itr[2, 1]
       end
(0.17086814961424035, 0.8039107344409738)

julia> let itr = Iterators.product(Dict(:a => 1, :b => 2), [1, 2, 3])
           itr[:b, 3]
       end
(2, 3)

julia> let itr = Iterators.product()
           itr[]
       end
()
```
and something that rightfully errors:
```julia
julia> let itr = Iterators.product(rand(3), (x for x in 1:10 if rand(Bool)))
           itr[1, 2]
       end
ERROR: MethodError: no method matching getindex(::Base.Generator{Base.Iterators.Filter{var"#10#11", UnitRange{Int64}}, typeof(identity)}, ::Int64)
```
Here, I've implemented the most straightforward case of indexing hoping to solicit comments on what this functionality should offer. For instance, this does not currently support things like linear indexing:
```julia
julia> Iterators.product([:a, :b, :c], ['i', 'j', 'k'])[2, 3]
(:b, 'k')

julia> Iterators.product([:a, :b, :c], ['i', 'j', 'k'])[2]
ERROR: BoundsError: attempt to access Tuple{} at index [1]
```
which I think is okay because this object isn't an abstract array. Thoughts on this function getindex(prod::ProductIterator, indices...)
    return _prod_getindex(prod.vectors, indices...)
end
_prod_getindex(::Tuple{}) = ()
function _prod_getindex(p_vecs::Tuple, indices...)
    return (
        first(prod.vectors)[first(indices)],
        _prod_getindex(Base.tail(p_vecs), Base.tail(indices)...)
    )
end

inds...)`","user":{"login":"MasonProtter","id":29157027,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI5MTU3MDI3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/29157027?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter","html_url":"https://github.com/MasonProtter","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Currently, `ProductIterator` has a rather artificial limitation of not supporting `getindex`, even though there's nothing about `ProductIterator` itself that should preclude it from supporting this function.

It's true that `ProductIterator` **may** contain iterators that don't support indexing, but if that's the case, then it should be the job of the enclosed iterators to throw an error saying they don't support `getindex`, not `ProductIterator`.

Here's some examples of things this enables:
```julia
julia> let itr = Iterators.product(rand(3), rand(3))
           itr[2, 1]
       end
(0.17086814961424035, 0.8039107344409738)

julia> let itr = Iterators.product(Dict(:a => 1, :b => 2), [1, 2, 3])
           itr[:b, 3]
       end
(2, 3)

julia> let itr = Iterators.product()
           itr[]
       end
()
```
and something that rightfully errors:
```julia
julia> let itr = Iterators.product(rand(3), (x for x in 1:10 if rand(Bool)))
           itr[1, 2]
       end
ERROR: MethodError: no method matching getindex(::Base.Generator{Base.Iterators.Filter{var"#10#11", UnitRange{Int64}}, typeof(identity)}, ::Int64)
```
Here, I've implemented the most straightforward case of indexing hoping to solicit comments on what this functionality should offer. For instance, this does not currently support things like linear indexing:
```julia
julia> Iterators.product([:a, :b, :c], ['i', 'j', 'k'])[2, 3]
(:b, 'k')

julia> Iterators.product([:a, :b, :c], ['i', 'j', 'k'])[2]
ERROR: BoundsError: attempt to access Tuple{} at index [1]
```
which I think is okay because this object isn't an abstract array. Thoughts on this inds...)`","user":{"login":"MasonProtter","id":29157027,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI5MTU3MDI3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/29157027?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter","html_url":"https://github.com/MasonProtter","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Currently, `ProductIterator` has a rather artificial limitation of not supporting `getindex`, even though there's nothing about `ProductIterator` itself that should preclude it from supporting this function.

It's true that `ProductIterator` **may** contain iterators that don't support indexing, but if that's the case, then it should be the job of the enclosed iterators to throw an error saying they don't support `getindex`, not `ProductIterator`.

Here's some examples of things this enables:
```julia
julia> let itr = Iterators.product(rand(3), rand(3))
           itr[2, 1]
       end
(0.17086814961424035, 0.8039107344409738)

julia> let itr = Iterators.product(Dict(:a => 1, :b => 2), [1, 2, 3])
           itr[:b, 3]
       end
(2, 3)

julia> let itr = Iterators.product()
           itr[]
       end
()
```
and something that rightfully errors:
```julia
julia> let itr = Iterators.product(rand(3), (x for x in 1:10 if rand(Bool)))
           itr[1, 2]
       end
ERROR: MethodError: no method matching getindex(::Base.Generator{Base.Iterators.Filter{var"#10#11", UnitRange{Int64}}, typeof(identity)}, ::Int64)
```
Here, I've implemented the most straightforward case of indexing hoping to solicit comments on what this functionality should offer. For instance, this does not currently support things like linear indexing:
```julia
julia> Iterators.product([:a, :b, :c], ['i', 'j', 'k'])[2, 3]
(:b, 'k')

julia> Iterators.product([:a, :b, :c], ['i', 'j', 'k'])[2]
ERROR: BoundsError: attempt to access Tuple{} at index [1]
```
which I think is okay because this object isn't an abstract array. Thoughts on this inds...)`","user":{"login":"MasonProtter","id":29157027,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI5MTU3MDI3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/29157027?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter","html_url":"https://github.com/MasonProtter","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Currently, `ProductIterator` has a rather artificial limitation of not supporting `getindex`, even though there's nothing about `ProductIterator` itself that should preclude it from supporting this function.

It's true that `ProductIterator` **may** contain iterators that don't support indexing, but if that's the case, then it should be the job of the enclosed iterators to throw an error saying they don't support `getindex`, not `ProductIterator`.

Here's some examples of things this enables:
```julia
julia> let itr = Iterators.product(rand(3), rand(3))
           itr[2, 1]
       end
(0.17086814961424035, 0.8039107344409738)

julia> let itr = Iterators.product(Dict(:a => 1, :b => 2), [1, 2, 3])
           itr[:b, 3]
       end
(2, 3)

julia> let itr = Iterators.product()
           itr[]
       end
()
```
and something that rightfully errors:
```julia
julia> let itr = Iterators.product(rand(3), (x for x in 1:10 if rand(Bool)))
           itr[1, 2]
       end
ERROR: MethodError: no method matching getindex(::Base.Generator{Base.Iterators.Filter{var"#10#11", UnitRange{Int64}}, typeof(identity)}, ::Int64)
```
Here, I've implemented the most straightforward case of indexing hoping to solicit comments on what this functionality should offer. For instance, this does not currently support things like linear indexing:
```julia
julia> Iterators.product([:a, :b, :c], ['i', 'j', 'k'])[2, 3]
(:b, 'k')

julia> Iterators.product([:a, :b, :c], ['i', 'j', 'k'])[2]
ERROR: BoundsError: attempt to access Tuple{} at index [1]
```
which I think is okay because this object isn't an abstract array. Thoughts on this It's true that `ProductIterator` **may** contain iterators that don't support indexing, but if that's the case, then it should be the job of the enclosed iterators to throw an error saying they don't support `getindex`, not `ProductIterator`.

Here's some examples of things this enables:

```julia

to get this output at the moment it is necessary to collect the ProductIterator. This is because PermutedDimsArray only accepts AbstractArrays and ProductIterator is not an AbstractArray. For this reason it might make sense to got the route

```julia
struct VectorProduct{T,N} <: AbstractArray{NTuple{N,T},N}
    vectors::NTuple{N,AbstractVector{T}}
end
product(vectors::Vector...) = VectorProduct(vectors)
```

inds...)`","user":{"login":"MasonProtter","id":29157027,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI5MTU3MDI3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/29157027?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter","html_url":"https://github.com/MasonProtter","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Currently, `ProductIterator` has a rather artificial limitation of not supporting `getindex`, even though there's nothing about `ProductIterator` itself that should preclude it from supporting this function.

It's true that `ProductIterator` **may** contain iterators that don't support indexing, but if that's the case, then it should be the job of the enclosed iterators to throw an error saying they don't support `getindex`, not `ProductIterator`.

Here's some examples of things this enables:
```julia
julia> let itr = Iterators.product(rand(3), rand(3))
           itr[2, 1]
       end
(0.17086814961424035, 0.8039107344409738)

julia> let itr = Iterators.product(Dict(:a => 1, :b => 2), [1, 2, 3])
           itr[:b, 3]
       end
(2, 3)

julia> let itr = Iterators.product()
           itr[]
       end
()
```
and something that rightfully errors:
```julia
julia> let itr = Iterators.product(rand(3), (x for x in 1:10 if rand(Bool)))
           itr[1, 2]
       end
ERROR: MethodError: no method matching getindex(::Base.Generator{Base.Iterators.Filter{var"#10#11", UnitRange{Int64}}, typeof(identity)}, ::Int64)
```
Here, I've implemented the most straightforward case of indexing hoping to solicit comments on what this functionality should offer. For instance, this does not currently support things like linear indexing:
```julia
julia> Iterators.product([:a, :b, :c], ['i', 'j', 'k'])[2, 3]
(:b, 'k')

julia> Iterators.product([:a, :b, :c], ['i', 'j', 'k'])[2]
ERROR: BoundsError: attempt to access Tuple{} at index [1]
```
which I think is okay because this object isn't an abstract array. Thoughts on this inds...)`","user":{"login":"MasonProtter","id":29157027,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI5MTU3MDI3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/29157027?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter","html_url":"https://github.com/MasonProtter","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Currently, `ProductIterator` has a rather artificial limitation of not supporting `getindex`, even though there's nothing about `ProductIterator` itself that should preclude it from supporting this function.

It's true that `ProductIterator` **may** contain iterators that don't support indexing, but if that's the case, then it should be the job of the enclosed iterators to throw an error saying they don't support `getindex`, not `ProductIterator`.

Here's some examples of things this enables:
```julia
julia> let itr = Iterators.product(rand(3), rand(3))
           itr[2, 1]
       end
(0.17086814961424035, 0.8039107344409738)

julia> let itr = Iterators.product(Dict(:a => 1, :b => 2), [1, 2, 3])
           itr[:b, 3]
       end
(2, 3)

julia> let itr = Iterators.product()
           itr[]
       end
()
```
and something that rightfully errors:
```julia
julia> let itr = Iterators.product(rand(3), (x for x in 1:10 if rand(Bool)))
           itr[1, 2]
       end
ERROR: MethodError: no method matching getindex(::Base.Generator{Base.Iterators.Filter{var"#10#11", UnitRange{Int64}}, typeof(identity)}, ::Int64)
```
Here, I've implemented the most straightforward case of indexing hoping to solicit comments on what this functionality should offer. For instance, this does not currently support things like linear indexing:
```julia
julia> Iterators.product([:a, :b, :c], ['i', 'j', 'k'])[2, 3]
(:b, 'k')

julia> Iterators.product([:a, :b, :c], ['i', 'j', 'k'])[2]
ERROR: BoundsError: attempt to access Tuple{} at index [1]
```
which I think is okay because this object isn't an abstract array. Thoughts on this inds...)`","user":{"login":"MasonProtter","id":29157027,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI5MTU3MDI3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/29157027?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter","html_url":"https://github.com/MasonProtter","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MasonProtter/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":3,"created_at":"2023-05-26T17:11:41Z","updated_at":"2023-05-29T17:14:05Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/49965","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/49965","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/49965.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/49965.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"Currently, `ProductIterator` has a rather artificial limitation of not supporting `getindex`, even though there's nothing about `ProductIterator` itself that should preclude it from supporting this function.

It's true that `ProductIterator` **may** contain iterators that don't support indexing, but if that's the case, then it should be the job of the enclosed iterators to throw an error saying they don't support `getindex`, not `ProductIterator`.

Here's some examples of things this enables:
```julia
julia> let itr = Iterators.product(rand(3), rand(3))
           itr[2, 1]
       end
(0.17086814961424035, 0.8039107344409738)

julia> let itr = Iterators.product(Dict(:a => 1, :b => 2), [1, 2, 3])
           itr[:b, 3]
       end
(2, 3)

julia> let itr = Iterators.product()
           itr[]
       end
()
```
and something that rightfully errors:
```julia
julia> let itr = Iterators.product(rand(3), (x for x in 1:10 if rand(Bool)))
           itr[1, 2]
       end
ERROR: MethodError: no method matching getindex(::Base.Generator{Base.Iterators.Filter{var"#10#11", UnitRange{Int64}}, typeof(identity)}, ::Int64)
```
Here, I've implemented the most straightforward case of indexing hoping to solicit comments on what this functionality should offer. For instance, this does not currently support things like linear indexing:
```julia
julia> Iterators.product([:a, :b, :c], ['i', 'j', 'k'])[2, 3]
(:b, 'k')

julia> Iterators.product([:a, :b, :c], ['i', 'j', 'k'])[2]
ERROR: BoundsError: attempt to access Tuple{} at index [1]
```
which I think is okay because this object isn't an abstract array. Thoughts on this direction?","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49965/reactions","total_count":1,"+1":1,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49965/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1567361442","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/49965#issuecomment-1567361442","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49965","id":1567361442,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5dbAmi","user":{"login":"FelixBenning","id":29106652,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI5MTA2NjUy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/29106652?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning","html_url":"https://github.com/FelixBenning","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-05-29T17:14:04Z","updated_at":"2023-05-29T17:14:04Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"maybe something like this to get the subtype of AbstractArray?
```julia
function _eltype_iterator_tuple(::Type{T}) where {T<:Tuple}
    return Tuple{ntuple(n -> eltype(fieldtype(T, n)), Base._counttuple(T))...}
end

struct ProductArray{T<:Tuple,Eltype,N} <: AbstractArray{Eltype,N}
    vectors::T
    ProductArray(vectors::T) where {T} = new{T,_eltype_iterator_tuple(T),Base._counttuple(T)}(vectors)
end

struct AllLinearIndexed end
struct GeneralIterators end

function _all_linear_indexed(::T) where {T<:Tuple}
    all(ntuple(
        n -> hasmethod(Base.getindex, (fieldtype(T, n), Int)),
        Base._counttuple(T)
    )) && return AllLinearIndexed()
    return GeneralIterators()
end
product(iterators...) = _product(_all_linear_indexed(iterators), iterators)
_product(::AllLinearIndexed, vectors) = ProductArray(vectors)
_product(::GeneralIterators, vectors) = Base.ProductIterator(vectors)
```
with a `const Product = Union{ProductArray,ProductIterator}` it might be possible to implement all methods at once

The goal is to enable users to do

```julia
julia> vec(PermutedDimsArray(Iterators.product(rand(3), 1:4), (2,1)))
12-element reshape(PermutedDimsArray(::Matrix{Tuple{Float64, Int64}}, (2, 1)), 12) with eltype Tuple{Float64, Int64}:
 (0.39059299961662275, 1)
 (0.39059299961662275, 2)
 (0.39059299961662275, 3)
 (0.39059299961662275, 4)
 (0.1864604429198029, 1)
 (0.1864604429198029, 2)
 (0.1864604429198029, 3)
 (0.1864604429198029, 4)
 (0.3248860864098778, 1)
 (0.3248860864098778, 2)
 (0.3248860864098778, 3)
 (0.3248860864098778, 4)
````

for this `ProductIterator` would need to be a subtype of `AbstractArray`, which it can not be in general. This Pull request introduces the `ProductArray` as a complement to the `ProductIterator`  and uses traits to decide which to use when `product` is called. This decision might be possible to generalize further. Using a type union the existing implementations for `ProductIterator` are then all reused for `ProductArray` (only that it also inherits functionality from `AbstractArray`.

I am not very familiar with the internals of juila, so my usage of Unions might not be performance optimal.

The same idea could in principle also be applied to `Flatten` with the same from #49965. The goal is to enable users to do

```julia
julia> vec(PermutedDimsArray(Iterators.product(rand(3), 1:4), (2,1)))
12-element reshape(PermutedDimsArray(::Matrix{Tuple{Float64, Int64}}, (2, 1)), 12) with eltype Tuple{Float64, Int64}:
 (0.39059299961662275, 1)
 (0.39059299961662275, 2)
 (0.39059299961662275, 3)
 (0.39059299961662275, 4)
 (0.1864604429198029, 1)
 (0.1864604429198029, 2)
 (0.1864604429198029, 3)
 (0.1864604429198029, 4)
 (0.3248860864098778, 1)
 (0.3248860864098778, 2)
 (0.3248860864098778, 3)
 (0.3248860864098778, 4)
````

for this `ProductIterator` would need to be a subtype of `AbstractArray`, which it can not be in general. This Pull request introduces the `ProductArray` as a complement to the `ProductIterator`  and uses traits to decide which to use when `product` is called. This decision might be possible to generalize further. Using a type union the existing implementations for `ProductIterator` are then all reused for `ProductArray` (only that it also inherits functionality from `AbstractArray`.

I am not very familiar with the internals of juila, so my usage of Unions might not be performance optimal.

The same idea could in principle also be applied to `Flatten` with the same goal.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49988/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49988/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1567487203","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/49988#issuecomment-1567487203","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49988","id":1567487203,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5dbfTj","user":{"login":"FelixBenning","id":29106652,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI5MTA2NjUy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/29106652?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning","html_url":"https://github.com/FelixBenning","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FelixBenning/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-05-29T20:21:52Z","updated_at":"2023-05-29T20:21:52Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"The compiler neither likes

MWE:
```julia
using SpecialFunctions, Zygote
function maternKernel(
    nu,
    d
)
    if d > 0
        x = sqrt(2* nu*d)
        return 2^(1-nu)/gamma(nu) * x^nu * besselk(nu,x)
    else
        return 1 # variance
    end
    throw(ArgumentError("the squared Norm should never be negative!"))
end
Zygote.withgradient(x->maternKernel(1.,x), 0.)
```

interesting that this is valid syntax in Julia and Lua:

``` jl
function rec()
    return rec()
end
```

The difference is that in Lua, when you call `rec()` it will stare at you and engage your motherboard's built-in space heater until you ctrl-C out. In Julia:

``` jl
julia> rec()
ERROR: stack overflow
 in rec at none:2 (repeats 80000 times)
```

So the stack 80000 things deep. That's interesting.

Why does this matter? I'm not sure. But some people care a lot:

http://www.lua.org/pil/6.3.html

It was inspired by this question https://discourse.julialang.org/t/iterator-over-upper-triangular-part-of-cartesian-product/63118, where it appeared that `combinations` from `Combinatorics.jl` was apparently very inefficient. So I tried to generalize the "pairs" algorithm proposed in that thread and ended up using recursion:

```julia
function combinations(x,n)
    if n <= 0
    	return ( () )
    end
    if n== 1
    	return x
    end
    if n==2 # this case is unneccessary
        return ( (a,b) for (k,a) in enumerate(x) for b in Iterators.drop(x, k) )
    end
    return ( 
	(a, b...) 
	for (k,a) in enumerate(x) 
		for b in combinations(Iterators.drop(x,k), n-1) 
        )
end
```
With the `n==2` case you get
```julia
@btime all(x-> gcd(x...) ==1, comb( (1,2,3,4,5,6 ), 2 ))
53.330 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
```
If you leave out the `n==2` case you get

```julia
@btime all(x-> gcd(x...) ==1, comb( (1,2,3,4,5,6 ), 2 ))
 742.421 ns (37 allocations: 3.08 KiB)
```

Since `combinations(x, 1)` just returns `x`, this could be optimized into the `n==2` case with tail end optimization I think. But writing this algorithm without recursion is harder than I thought. So I think this might be an interesting toy example","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1141119018/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
